description_long: This is a large, cylindrical metal barrel with a dark, weathered exterior and visible rust streaks running down from the top rim. The surface is matte and rough, showing signs of age and exposure to the elements, with patches of corrosion and discoloration. A hazardous material warning label featuring a flame symbol is affixed to one side, indicating the contents may be flammable. The top of the barrel has two small, round bungs or caps, one of which is metallic and the other appears darker, likely for filling or emptying the barrel. The structure is reinforced with two horizontal metal bands, giving it added strength and rigidity. This type of barrel is commonly found in industrial or urban outdoor settings, often used for storing chemicals, oil, or waste.
description: A large, weathered metal barrel with rust streaks, a flammable warning label, and reinforced bands, typically used for hazardous materials in industrial or urban environments.
description_view_0: Front view showing the flammable warning label, rust streaks, and both bungs on the top.
description_view_1: Side view with visible rust and corrosion, no label present, and both bungs visible on the top.
description_view_2: Rear side with heavy weathering, no label, and both bungs visible on the top.
description_view_3: Opposite side to the label, showing a mostly unmarked, rusted surface and both bungs on the top.
attribute_banding_or_reinforcement: metal hoops at top and bottom
